agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Real-time courier tracking feature for customer-facing delivery experience. Two internal stakeholder teams present directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, Operations demands no location sharing. This is a fundamental business strategy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Customer Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier Location","definition":"The GPS coordinates of a delivery driver during an active delivery, collected from the driver's mobile device."},{"term":"Real-Time Tracking","definition":"Displaying courier position to the customer with frequent updates (typically every few seconds) during active delivery."},{"term":"Active Delivery","definition":"The period between a courier picking up a package and completing the delivery at the destination."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a derived value that can communicate delivery progress without exposing raw location."},{"term":"Delivery Status","definition":"A discrete state (e.g., Picked Up, En Route, Nearby, Delivered) that communicates progress without granular location."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) — exact GPS exposes this and may trigger customer complaints or driver harassment","expected_behavior":"Cannot define until contradiction is resolved — this scenario illustrates why the conflict matters"},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (multi-drop, personal stop) — GPS visibility creates customer anxiety and driver pressure","expected_behavior":"Cannot define until contradiction is resolved"},{"scenario":"Courier is near delivery address but serving another customer first — exact location creates false expectation of imminent delivery","expected_behavior":"Cannot define until contradiction is resolved"},{"scenario":"Post-delivery period — courier location must never be visible after delivery is complete regardless of resolution","expected_behavior":"Location sharing ceases immediately upon delivery completion — this is a privacy invariant independent of the contradiction"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High stakes in both directions. Customer Success claims tracking is a competitive differentiator (revenue/retention). Operations claims lack of driver privacy is causing driver churn (driver acquisition costs $3K-$8K per driver in logistics industry). Both are revenue-critical — losing customers OR losing drivers collapses the business.","operational":"Driver retention directly affects delivery capacity and service reliability. If drivers leave, no amount of customer-facing features matters. Conversely, if customers churn due to perceived lack of transparency, driver supply is irrelevant.","user":"Two user groups with opposing needs: customers want visibility and control; couriers want dignity and privacy. Both are 'users' of this system.","risk":"CRITICAL: Implementing either requirement as stated will actively harm the other stakeholder group. Shipping exact GPS satisfies customers but accelerates driver attrition. Hiding location entirely satisfies drivers but may erode customer trust. There is also a legal/regulatory dimension — GDPR and similar frameworks may restrict sharing worker location data with third parties without explicit consent.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want delivery visibility; lack of tracking may reduce trust and increase support contacts ('where is my package?')"},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Feel surveilled by exact GPS sharing; driver retention is already a problem; this is a labor relations and dignity issue"},{"group":"Customer Success Team","impact":"KPIs tied to customer satisfaction and retention; they see tracking as essential to their metrics"},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"KPIs tied to driver retention, fleet capacity, and delivery reliability; they see GPS sharing as actively harmful to their metrics"},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Worker location data sharing has GDPR, labor law, and privacy regulation implications that neither team has raised"},{"group":"Executive Leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic business direction decision, not a feature decision"}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because the spec contains a direct, irreconcilable contradiction between two stakeholder teams, both claiming non-negotiable status. No engineering solution can satisfy both requirements as stated. Additionally, there is a missing legal/regulatory dimension that neither team has addressed. Until executive leadership resolves the strategic direction, defining acceptance criteria would be irresponsible — we would be encoding one team's preference as truth and alienating the other.","high_confidence_areas":["Courier location must never be shared after delivery is complete — both sides would agree on this","Any solution must respect applicable privacy regulations for worker location data","The conflict is real and cannot be resolved by engineering alone"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether any location data should be shared with customers","What granularity of location sharing (if any) is acceptable to drivers","Whether a compromise (e.g., ETA-only, zone-based, status-based) would satisfy Customer Success","Legal and regulatory constraints on sharing worker GPS with customers","Whether driver consent is required and how it interacts with employment terms"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their stakeholders' positions","Driver retention is genuinely being affected by location sharing (Operations claim)","Customer satisfaction is genuinely tied to location visibility (Customer Success claim)","No executive decision has already been made that one team is unaware of"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers. These requirements are mutually exclusive as stated. This is not a technical ambiguity — it is a fundamental business strategy conflict about whether courier location is a customer-facing asset or a protected worker privacy concern. This MUST be resolved by executive leadership before any engineering work begins.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Customer Success Team","Operations Team","Executive Leadership","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["A: Full GPS transparency (Customer Success position) — show exact coordinates. Risk: accelerated driver churn.","B: No location sharing (Operations position) — hide all location data. Risk: customer churn, increased support volume.","C: Compromise — share abstracted progress (ETA, delivery status, zone-based proximity like 'nearby') without exact GPS. Requires validation with BOTH teams.","D: Driver-controlled consent — let couriers opt in/out of location sharing, with incentive structure. Requires product design and driver input.","E: Contextual sharing — show approximate location only in final delivery mile or last N minutes. Balances both concerns partially."],"recommendation":"Option C or D. The logistics industry has largely converged on abstracted tracking (ETA + status updates + approximate proximity) rather than raw GPS — see Uber Eats, DoorDash, Amazon. This satisfies the customer's actual need (knowing when my delivery arrives) without the surveillance concern. However, I refuse to encode this as acceptance criteria until both teams and executive leadership align. Recommend a stakeholder alignment meeting before any implementation begins."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No legal or regulatory review has been cited by either team. Sharing worker GPS coordinates with third-party customers has significant implications under GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), and emerging worker surveillance legislation. This is not optional context — it could make Option A legally impermissible regardless of business preference.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers/Drivers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage legal counsel before defining requirements","Review applicable jurisdictions where couriers operate","Assess whether driver employment contracts address location data sharing"],"recommendation":"Block all requirement finalization until legal review is complete. If the company operates in the EU, GDPR Article 6 and worker privacy directives likely apply."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Direct contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements makes it impossible to define correct behavior","business_rule_broken":"Cannot encode business rules when the business has not decided what the rules are"},{"violation":"Missing legal/regulatory analysis on worker location data sharing","business_rule_broken":"Privacy and compliance constraints must be established before feature requirements — law supersedes both teams' preferences"}]}
